message:
test,bench(wa-22x4r): LabRuntime port regression guard + time-dependent comparison bench

Closes wa-22x4r acceptance criteria that were not yet backed by
executable evidence:

1. tests/wa_22x4r_no_tokio_test_in_supported_paths.rs (173 lines,
   2 tests): walks `crates/` and `frankenterm/`, asserts no `.rs`
   file contains a line whose trimmed content begins with
   `#[tokio::test`. Companion test asserts ≥20 `*_labruntime.rs` port
   files still exist so accidental deletion of the port work is
   caught. Prior ports by other agents have already migrated every
   active `#[tokio::test]` attribute in supported-path code; this
   test pins that invariant. Both tests pass.

2. benches/test_execution.rs (5 bench scenarios, 177 lines): pins
   the bead's headline claim that LabRuntime wins on time-dependent
   tests. Measures `tokio_sleep_1ms`, `tokio_sleep_10ms` (real wall
   clock via tokio::time::sleep + block_on) against matching
   `lab_sleep_{1,10}ms` (asupersync::time::budget_sleep under
   LabRuntime auto-advance — virtual time, no real wait). Adds
   `lab_finish_chain_1ms_x5` to measure per-timer overhead for
   chained awaits. All 5 smoke-test `--test` runs succeed; bench
   manifest lands at target/criterion/wa-bench-manifest-test_execution.json.

The existing labruntime_overhead.rs already covers setup/teardown,
oracle cost, spawn cost, DPOR exploration cost, and trivial-future
block_on comparison. test_execution.rs adds only the time-dependent
speedup scenario the bead explicitly calls out; deliberately narrow
scope to avoid duplicating labruntime_overhead's surface.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -314,6 +314,11 @@ name = "labruntime_overhead"
 harness = false
 required-features = ["asupersync-runtime"]
 
+[[bench]]
+name = "test_execution"
+harness = false
+required-features = ["asupersync-runtime"]
+
 [[bench]]
 name = "outcome_adapter"
 harness = false
